text:
CHAR 
LENGTH: 
6 (starting in 2018, length is 2) 
SOURCE: 
CMS (HPMS files) 
VALUES: 
For 2007–2017 data — this is a binary character string where each digit is a 1 or 0 to accommodate a 
variety of types of drugs. For example, if the value = 110000 (1s in the 1st and 2nd digits), then generic 
and non-preferred brand drugs are covered on this tier during the pre-ICL phase. 
1 in 1st digit = Non-Preferred Brand 
1 in 2nd digit = Generic 
1 in 3rd digit = Preferred Generic 
1 in 4th digit =
